content.text:
[cron:9d037df1-1600-429d-888b-66aeea074ce3 moltbook-test-cycle] Run the Moltbook testing cycle. Read the drafts from moltbook_drafts.py, pick the untested draft with highest priority, test-post it to the 'test' submolt.

Execute: cd C:/Users/Zwmar/.openclaw/workspace && python moltbook_client.py test <draft_idx>

If the post succeeds (returns a post ID), read the post content and score it:
- Does it fit the Moltbook voice? (first-person, counterintuitive, ~400-800 words)
- Is the title compelling enough to click?
- Does it match current hot topics on Moltbook?

Then write the result to memory/moltbook-testing.md:
- Draft number, title, post ID, test result (pass/fail), timestamp

If all drafts have been tested, mark them as tested in the file and skip.

Report what was tested and your assessment.
Current time: Tuesday, March 24th, 2026 — 10:00 AM (America/New_York) / 2026-03-24 14:00 UTC

Return your summary as plain text; it will be delivered automatically. If the task explicitly calls for messaging a specific external recipient, note who/where it should go instead of sending it yourself.
